sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
seg = current_frame.segmentation
nodes = seg['nodes']
magenta = [n for n in nodes if n['color'] == 'M']
if magenta:
    m = magenta[0]
    r, c = m['boundary'][0]
    print(f'Clicking magenta at ({r}, {c})')
    action([{'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': r, 'col': c})
</parameter>
<parameter=world_model>
{"world_model": "Board has objects along a diagonal path. Clicking the magenta center causes chain movement along the diagonal.
goal_model: Need to continue clicking magenta centers to move objects along the chain until something completes.
action_model: MOUSE on magenta center triggers chain movement.
recent_findings: Clicking (47,17) moved white arrow to purple square's old spot, purple moved toward gray diamond. Then clicking (53,22) moved objects further down the chain.
open_questions: What happens when clicking the next magenta center.
plan: Find the new magenta center and click it.
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
